steps.n: These are special letters that appear at the start of some chapters of the Quran. They are read as individual letters: 'Ha' and 'Meem'.

steps.m: Ha Meem (special letters at start of chapter)
steps.n: The long mark (ٓ) shows these are special chapter-opening letters whose full meaning is known only to Allah.
Connected the root letters with basic past tense vowels
Changes from the action of writing to the thing that is written
Adds 'and' to connect this word to what came before it

steps.m: to read or recite out loud
steps.n: This forms the noun 'Quran' - that which is recited
Changed the vowels to turn the action into a thing. Like changing 'read' to 'reading' in English.
Added ان to show this is a special, complete collection of readings/recitations

steps.m: as a complete book for recitation
steps.n: Added ًا at the end to show this describes how Allah made it (referring back to جَعَلْنٰهُ in the ayah)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic noun
Added ِيّ to show this describes something related to Arabs
Added ًا to show this is describing the Quran mentioned earlier in the verse

steps.m: surely perhaps/definitely so that (connects to تَعْقِلُونَ)
steps.n: Adding shadda on first letter makes the meaning stronger and more certain

steps.m: surely perhaps you all/definitely so that you all (connects to تَعْقِلُونَ)
steps.n: The suffix كُمْ means 'you all' (masculine plural)

steps.m: you all understand (so that you all may understand)
steps.n: Added ون to show multiple people are being addressed. This word follows لَّعَلَّكُمْ (so that you may) in the ayah.

The و connects this word to what came before it, like saying 'and'

steps.m: and indeed it (referring to the Quran)
steps.n: The هُۥ shows what we're talking about (the Quran), like saying 'it' in English. This connects to the next part of the ayah which talks about the Quran being in the Mother of the Book.

steps.n: This is a basic preposition showing that something exists inside or within something else. Here it shows that 'it' (the Quran) exists within 'the Mother of the Book'.

steps.m: in (location within أُمِّ ٱلْكِتٰبِ - the Mother of the Book)
steps.n: The ى with ٓ (alif) is just a spelling variation of ي that doesn't change the meaning

steps.m: to be the source or origin of something

steps.n: The ُ (damma) makes it into a noun meaning the source/origin of something
Added kasra at the end to show this word owns or is the source of what comes after it
Connected the root letters with fatha to form the basic past tense verb
Changes from a verb (wrote) to a noun (book/writing) by adding alif

steps.n: The ٱلْ makes it definite ('the'), and the ِ shows it's in the genitive case
This is a preposition indicating presence or possession

Added نَا to show this means 'with us' or 'near us'. In this ayah, it's describing where something is located - in our presence.

Adding the double mark (shadda) and two dots (tanween) makes it mean 'very high' instead of just 'high'
Adding 'la' at start makes it mean 'surely/truly/indeed high'

Added the fatha mark (َ) above the أ to show how to pronounce it correctly as 'anna'
Added damma and sukun to make the basic command form
Added تُ at the end to show past tense for 'I'
Changed from 'I' to 'you all' by adding تم at the end

steps.m: people who stand together (as a community)

The word 'min' (from) connects two words together - it links 'we sent' with 'prophet'

The root letters ك-و-ن become كَانَ (he was) by changing و to ا - this is a common change in words with و in the middle

Changed the verb into a noun for the thing above us

Added vowel marks (fatha) and the small alif (ٰ) to help with pronunciation. The small alif shows that the ى is pronounced like a long 'aa' sound.
Forms the basic noun for the physical thing related to rising/appearing - the back (since it rises up from the body)
Changes from single 'back' to multiple 'backs' by changing middle vowels to 'u' sounds
Adds -i ending to show these backs belong to something/someone
Adds هِ (his/its) to show possession. The ۦ is just a pronunciation mark. This word follows عَلَىٰ (upon) in the phrase 'upon its backs'.
Added damma (ُ) and shadda (ّ) to complete the word. This word connects two actions in sequence - first 'so that you may sit' then 'remember'.
ت at start shows it's about 'you' doing the action
The ُ at the end shows this is a normal present tense verb
The وا at the end shows we're talking to multiple people (like changing 'you do' to 'you all do' in English)
Added vowel marks to connect the root letters
Added ة to make it refer to one specific blessing. This word is what is being remembered in تَذْكُرُوا۟ (you remember)
Added fatha at the end to show this is the object being remembered
Connected the root letters with fatha to create basic past tense form
The doubled middle letter shows this is someone's main role or characteristic - being a master and caretaker
Adding kasra shows this word belongs to or is connected to something else

steps.m: your (all of you) master/caretaker (referring to Allah as the Lord of the people being addressed)
steps.n: Adding كُمْ shows this refers to multiple people being spoken to - 'your' in plural form
This word connects two actions in time - when you sit (تَسْتَوُوا) and when you are settled (ٱسْتَوَيْتُمْ). It shows the second action happens after the first one.
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb
Added است at the beginning to show something reaching its final state or completion (like settling into place)
Added silent ي at the end to show the word is in jussive mood (like when saying 'do not settle')
Added ا at start to show this is a Form VIII verb (like other verbs that start with است-)
The base word عَلَى (upon) changes its final ى to ي before adding pronouns. This is just a spelling change that doesn't affect the meaning.

steps.m: upon him/it (referring to ظُهُورِهِ - its back)
steps.n: Added هِ (him/it) to show what/who is being referred to. In this ayah, it refers back to the animal's back (ظُهُورِهِ) that was mentioned.
Added present tense marker ت with fatha to show 'you' are doing the action
Adds a 'u' ending to show it's a fact/statement (not a command or wish)
Plural masculine ending changes subject from singular to plural

steps.m: and you all say (so that you say سُبْحٰنَ)
steps.n: Adds 'and' to connect this with what comes before, showing this is what you should say after settling on the animal's back. This introduces the praise (سُبْحٰنَ) that follows.

steps.m: to be free from faults/to glorify

steps.n: Root letters joined together with vowels to make a noun meaning 'glory'
Adds ان to make it stronger, like saying 'ultimate glory'
Adds fatha (َ) to show it's being used as an object or complement
Starting with the basic demonstrative pronoun that forms the base of the relative pronoun
Changed from 'this' to 'which/who' by adding ي at the end - this makes it connect words together

steps.m: the one who/which (refers to what Allah has made subservient)
steps.n: Added ال to make it definite. In this ayah, it refers back to what Allah has made subservient for us to ride on.

steps.m: the one who/which (refers to what Allah has made subservient)
steps.n: Added proper Quranic spelling with hamza wasl (ٱ), shadda on ل, kasra on ذ, and alif maqsura (ى) at the end

steps.m: to control or make useful for service

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past

steps.m: he made completely subservient (referring to Allah in this ayah)
steps.n: Doubling the middle letter shows Allah's complete power in making things useful for humans
Added fatha to show it connects to what follows
Added نَا which means 'us'. This word shows who is speaking in تَقُولُوا۟ (you all say)
The attention-drawing particle ها is written with a special mark (alif khanjariya) above it to show it's connected to what follows
Added ذَ which points to something nearby. Together هٰذَ means 'look at this thing here'

steps.m: this (referring to what was just mentioned)
steps.n: Added ا at the end to complete the demonstrative pronoun. In this ayah, it refers back to the transportation that Allah has provided
Basic particle that can mean either 'not' or 'what' depending on context. Here it means 'not' since it's negating the following verb كُنَّا (we were).
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it
Added vowels (fatha) to make it past tense 'he was'
Changed vowels to prepare for 'we' ending
Added نا with shadda to show 'we' as the subject
Word is already in final form with 'we' suffix
Added fatha to show the vowel sound
Added هُ at the end to show who/what is being referred to. This connects to هٰذَا (this) mentioned earlier in the ayah, referring to the transport Allah provided.
Added the prolongation mark (ۥ) to show the extended pronunciation of the final vowel

steps.m: to have power or control over something

steps.n: Adding vowel marks makes it a proper past tense verb
Added مُ at start to mean 'one who' and changed vowels to show having power over something
Added ين to make it plural (more than two)

steps.m: those who have power/control (object of كُنَّا)
steps.n: Added َ at the end to show this describes what 'we were' (كُنَّا) in the phrase 'we were not able to control this'
This is a word that emphasizes what comes after it, like saying 'definitely' or 'certainly'
The و at the beginning connects this with what came before it

steps.m: and indeed we (to رَبِّنَا - our Lord)
steps.n: The آ at the end means 'we'. This word connects to رَبِّنَا (our Lord) in the ayah, showing who we are returning to.
This is the basic form of the preposition that shows direction or destination
The small alif (ىٰ) at the end is just a writing variation that doesn't change the meaning. This preposition connects to 'our Lord' (رَبِّنَا) to show where we are returning to.

steps.m: to care for, nurture, raise up

steps.n: Doubling the ب makes it stronger - shows complete mastery/lordship
The kasra (ِ) is added because this word comes after إِلَىٰ (to)
Adding نَا shows this is our Lord. The whole phrase إِلَىٰ رَبِّنَا means 'to our Lord'

steps.m: to turn over or change direction

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Added اِن at beginning to show something happening to someone/something rather than someone doing it
Changed from a past-tense verb (he transformed) to show the person being transformed
Added ُونَ to show we're talking about multiple people
Added لَ at beginning to emphasize certainty. This word relates to رَبِّنَا (our Lord) in the ayah, showing who they are returning to.

steps.m: to make something into something else

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to show this happened in the past. This word shows someone actively changing one thing into another.
Adding وا changes it from one person (he) to many people (they) doing the action together

steps.m: and they made (something into something for لَهُ)
steps.n: Adding وَ connects this action to what came before. In this ayah, it shows what 'they' did in relation to لَهُ (for Him/it)
Added fatha to show this is a preposition meaning 'to' or 'for'
Added هُ at the end to show this is 'for him'. This word connects to جَعَلُوا (they made) in this ayah, showing what was made for him.
Added a small و mark (ۥ) after هُ which is just a pronunciation helper and doesn't change the meaning

This is a basic preposition that shows something comes from or is taken from something else. Here it connects 'they made' (جَعَلُوا) with 'His servants' (عِبَادِهِۦ) to show they took or assigned something from among His servants.
Connected the root letters with fatha to form the basic past tense verb
Changes from verb 'to serve' into plural noun meaning 'servants'
Added kasra to show this word is possessed by something
Added هِۦ to show the servants belong to Him
The root meaning 'divide' becomes a noun meaning 'a part' or 'portion' - here it shows what portion of servants they assigned

steps.m: a part (object of جَعَلُوا - they made)
steps.n: Shows this word is the object of جَعَلُوا (they made/assigned) in the ayah
This is the basic form of the particle that connects parts of a sentence, like saying 'that' in English
Adding the shadda makes this word stronger, changing it from a simple connector to a word that emphasizes what comes after it. In this ayah, it emphasizes that mankind (الإنسان) is ungrateful.
Root meaning 'friendly' became a collective noun meaning all humans
Changes from general 'humankind' to mean one specific human
Added ٱلْ to make it specific

steps.m: the human being (object of جَعَلُوا - they made)
steps.n: Added َ at the end to show this is the object of the verb جَعَلُوا (they made)
Changed the pattern to show someone who does this action intensively or habitually
The لَ prefix adds emphasis, meaning 'indeed/certainly/verily'

steps.m: truly/indeed a constant denier/rejecter (referring to mankind in general)
steps.n: The tanween (ٌ) shows this is describing mankind (الإنسان) in general terms
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the basic past tense verb
This form shows someone or something that actively makes things clear
The doubled م adds emphasis to show something that makes things very clear

steps.m: a very clear one (describing something that makes things obvious)
steps.n: Adds tanween dammah to make the word indefinite

This is a special word that connects two parts of a question, showing doubt between two choices. In this ayah, it connects to an implied previous question, suggesting 'Has Allah taken...?' as a way to challenge false beliefs.
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
The ت combines with أ to make a stronger/doubled sound, showing this is a more intense form of taking/adopting
This word combines two parts: مِن meaning 'from' and مَا meaning 'what'

steps.m: from what (subject of يَخْلُقُ - he creates)
steps.n: The ن of مِن changes to م and gets a shadda because it comes before another م. This is a common pronunciation rule in Arabic to make words flow better.
Connected the root letters together with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Added يَ at the beginning to show this is happening now. Changed the middle vowel to sukun and the last two vowels to damma to match the present tense pattern.

steps.m: to create offspring, to produce children

steps.n: Changes from 'making children' to mean specifically 'daughters' by adding -aat ending

steps.m: to be pure, select the best

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a past tense verb

Added و with fatha at beginning to mean 'and'

steps.m: and He chose you all (as His special ones)
steps.n: Added كُم (kum) at the end to show 'you all' as the ones being chosen. The ى (alif maqsurah) is part of the root word, not an extension.
Adding vowels َ and ِ creates the past tense verb form
This is incorrect - 'بَنِي' means 'sons' (plural) and 'بَنِينَ' is another form of the plural 'sons' when it comes in certain grammar positions. The word for 'he built' is 'بَنَى'

Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added shadda to make the action stronger and more intensive

Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic word meaning 'one'

steps.m: one (subject of بُشِّرَ - was given news)
steps.n: Added damma at the end to show this is the subject receiving the action of being given news

steps.m: one of them (subject of بُشِّرَ - was given news)
steps.n: Added هُم which means 'them', creating the meaning 'one of them'. This entire phrase is the subject receiving the action of being given news.
This is the basic interrogative particle meaning 'what'

steps.m: to hit, strike, give an example

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to show this is a past tense verb. In this ayah, it means 'gave an example' rather than physically hitting, as shown by the word مَثَلًا (example) that comes after it.

steps.n: The ان ending makes it mean 'full of mercy'
Changed alif to alif maqsurah (ٰ) which is just a writing variation with no meaning change
Added 'the' (ال) at the start to make it mean 'The Most Merciful' instead of just 'merciful', and doubled the ر sound with a shadda mark
Added لِ at beginning to mean 'to/for' and ِ at end to show connection
Added fathas to connect the letters and form a noun meaning 'example'

Changed root letters into a noun meaning 'face'. This word is the subject of ظَلَّ (became/remained) in this ayah.
Adds 'u' sound at end to show this word is doing the action (subject)
Adds 3rd person masculine singular possessive pronoun. This word is the subject of the verb ظَلَّ (became/remained) in the nominative case.
Added mu- prefix and shadda at end to show something becoming very black/dark
Added ًا to show this word is describing وَجْهُهُۥ (his face) in the ayah
This is a basic personal pronoun that doesn't come from a root
Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it. This pronoun 'he' is the subject of كَظِيمٌ (distressed) in this ayah.
Connected the letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Changed the word pattern to show someone filled with this feeling, similar to how we say 'angry' in English
This is the base interrogative particle that asks about people
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase to what came before it

steps.m: And is the one who (subject of يُنَشَّؤُا۟)

Added يُ at start to show it's happening now, and shadda to show it's being done to someone

Added the kasra (ِ) mark under ف to show how it's pronounced (fi), and wrote ي as ى since it's at the end of the word

steps.m: to decorate or beautify with jewelry/ornaments

Changed the vowel pattern and added ة to make it refer to a single ornament

Added ال ('the') at the beginning and ِ at the end because it comes after the word في (in/at)
Connected the letters and added vowel marks to make it a noun meaning 'other than'
Added ُ at the end to show this word is the subject (doer) in relation to the word مُبِينٍ (clear/manifest) that comes after it
Base concept of clarity or being evident

Added مُ at start to make it mean 'one who does the action' and changed vowels to show it's a describing word that means 'makes clear'
Added tanwin (two dots under) to show this word connects to and describes something else in the sentence

steps.m: to make something into something else, to set, to place

steps.m: and they made/set (referring to the disbelievers making angels into females)

Root letters shaped into pattern for 'female' noun - like changing 'female' into 'a female person'

steps.m: to witness or testify about what one has seen

steps.m: their creation (object of أَشَهِدُوا - did they witness?)

Connected the root letters with fatha to make a past tense verb
Changed vowels to show that the writing happened to something rather than someone doing the writing
Changed from past tense (it was written) to present tense (it is written) by adding تُ at the start and changing vowels
Added سَ at the beginning to show this will happen in the future

Changed from 'he witnessed' (past action) to the noun form meaning 'one testimony' (the thing itself)

Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Changed to passive voice by adding يُ at start and changing vowels to show the action is being done to someone

Starting with the basic demonstrative word ذا (this). The alif is written with a special mark (ٰ) in this form.

Added ًا to show this is the object being given in the phrase 'we gave them a book'

steps.m: from (emphasizing the start point of قَبْلِهِۦ 'before it')

Added هِۦ at the end to show possession. The ۦ is just a pronunciation mark.

steps.m: with/by/in it (referring to كِتٰبًا - a book)

steps.m: they are firmly holding/grasping (subject of فَهُم)

This is a basic particle that expresses hope or expectation for something to happen. It's like saying 'hopefully' or 'maybe' in English.

steps.m: they go/come back (in response to العَذَابِ/the punishment)

The middle letter و changes to ا when we add the past tense vowels. This is a common change with middle-weak verbs.

steps.m: to call out to someone, invite

steps.n: The alif and damma together make this a command form

steps.m: to care for, nurture, and help grow
one who cares for and nurtures completely
steps.n: The doubled middle letter shows this is someone who does complete nurturing and caring

steps.m: your Lord (being called upon in ٱدْعُ)
steps.n: Adding كَ at the end shows this Lord belongs to you. This word is what's being called upon when they say 'call/invoke' (ٱدْعُ)
Combines 'with/by' (بِ) and 'what' (مَا)

steps.m: to make a promise or covenant

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past. This word relates to رَبَّكَ (your Lord) in the ayah, showing who the covenant was made with.
Added vowels to connect the letters and make it into a preposition. The kasra and sukun pattern is common for prepositions.
Changed the sukun to fatha to prepare for adding the pronoun suffix
Added كَ to show this relates to 'you' (masculine singular). This word follows عَهِدَ (made a covenant) in the ayah, showing what was covenanted.

Adding نَا at the end shows this is about 'we/us'. In this ayah, the people are speaking to someone they call 'the magician'

steps.m: to guide or show the way

steps.n: Changed the root letters into a past tense verb by adding the vowel marks 'fatha' (َ) to mean 'he guided'
Added مُ at start and ت in middle to change from 'guiding' to 'being guided' - like changing from teacher to student
Added ون to show multiple people who are guided
Added لَ at start to emphasize that they are definitely guided
This is a time particle that shows one action happened right before another. In this ayah, it connects to the word كَشَفْنَا (we removed), showing when something happened.

steps.m: Then when (connects to كَشَفْنَا - we removed)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to show this happens right after something else. It's like saying 'and then' or 'so then' in English.
Added fatha (َ) after each letter to make the past tense form. The fatha marks show this happened in the past.
Added نَا at the end to show 'we' did the action. This word comes after فَلَمَّا (and when) in the ayah, showing when something happened.
Basic preposition meaning 'from' or 'away from'
Added هُمُ (them) to show who the action is happening to. This word connects to كَشَفْنَا (we removed) in this ayah, showing what was removed from them.
Changed from action to the result of that action (like changing 'punish' to 'punishment')

steps.m: the punishment (object of كَشَفْنَا - we removed)
steps.n: Added ٱلْ to make it specific and َ at the end to show it's receiving the action of being removed. This word is what was removed in the phrase 'we removed the punishment from them'
This is the basic form of the word that means 'when' in the past
Adding ا at the end changes it from just 'when' to mean 'when' with a sense of suddenness or surprise. In this ayah, it shows the sudden change in their behavior after Allah removed their punishment.
This is the basic form of the pronoun that refers to a single male person or thing
Adding مْ changes the pronoun from singular to plural. This pronoun is the subject (doer) of the verb يَنكُثُونَ (they break/violate)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb

Plural masculine suffix added to make subject 'they'

Changed the ي to ى which is common at the end of words. This doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: to stand/rise (or people who stand together)

steps.n: Root meaning 'stand' becomes a noun for a group of people who stand together as one community
Added kasra at the end to show this word is being possessed by someone
Added ه with kasra and small ي to show this belongs to Pharaoh (him)
Added fathas to make basic past tense form. This word introduces what Pharaoh says next in the ayah.
The weak letter و changes to ا, which is a common change in hollow verbs

steps.m: to stand together, gather, rise up

steps.n: Added vowel marks to connect the letters. The root meaning of standing/gathering together developed into meaning the people who gather as a community.
Adding يٰ at the start shows Pharaoh is calling out to his people. This is connected to فِرْعَوْنُ (Pharaoh) who is doing the calling out.

steps.m: O my people! (being called by فِرْعَوْنُ)
steps.n: Adding ِ at the end shows Pharaoh is speaking about his own people, making it more personal.
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and make it readable. The fatha on first letter, sukun on middle letter, and fatha on last letter show this is a complete verb.

steps.m: is it not? (asking about مُلْكُ مِصْرَ - the kingdom of Egypt)
steps.n: Adding 'أ' at the beginning turns this into a question. In this ayah, Pharaoh is asking rhetorically about his ownership of Egypt's kingdom.
Starting with the basic preposition ل which shows ownership or direction

steps.m: for me, belonging to me (connected to مُلْكُ/kingdom)
steps.n: Added ى at the end to show this means 'me'. This word connects to مُلْكُ (kingdom) to show that the kingdom belongs to Pharaoh.
The root letters م-ل-ك meaning 'rule/own' became a noun مُلْك meaning 'kingdom' by adding vowel marks. The damma at the end shows this is the main thing being talked about in Pharaoh's question.

Added vowels to create a noun meaning a defined region or territory
Added fatha at the end to show Egypt is the object being possessed in the phrase 'kingdom of Egypt'
This is a demonstrative pronoun used to point to something feminine that is near. The ٰ (alif) makes the word longer, and the ِ shows it's connected to something after it.
The وَ at the start means 'and', connecting this phrase to what came before it
Added vowels to connect the letters and make it into a noun for a single river

Added the definite article ال (written as ٱلْ) to specify these particular rivers

Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Changed from past to present tense by adding تَ prefix and changing middle vowel to kasra
Basic preposition showing where something comes from, starts, or showing a relationship between things
Added kasra (ِ) under م to show how to pronounce it correctly as 'min'
Basic concept of being underneath something
Added vowel marks to make it a usable word
Added kasra to prepare for possession suffix

steps.m: beneath me (in Pharaoh's boastful speech about the rivers flowing beneath him)
steps.n: Added ي with elongation mark to show possession by the speaker (Pharaoh)
This is a question word that turns a statement into a question, like adding a question mark in English
Added فَ which shows this question follows from what was said before

steps.m: so do you not? (connected to تُبْصِرُونَ - you see)
steps.n: Added لا which makes the question negative. This whole phrase is connected to the word تُبْصِرُونَ (you see) that comes after it, asking 'so do you not see?'
Added basic vowels to connect the root letters together
Changes from past tense 'he saw' to present tense 'you see'
Added ونَ at the end to show multiple people are doing the action

This is the basic form of the personal pronoun used to refer to yourself
Added alif at the end to make the pronoun stronger and clearer

steps.m: I (strongly emphasized, speaking about Pharaoh in this context)

Changes from verb meaning to noun meaning of 'good'

Added emphasis by doubling the م with a shadda
This is the basic attention-getting part that starts many pointing words
The ذ is added to show pointing to something specific
The ا is added at the end to show we're pointing to something close by (this) rather than far away (that)
The base word ذا means 'this'. Adding the ى and ِ at the end makes it ready to connect with other words

steps.m: who/which/that (referring to هٰذَا - this - from previous phrase)
steps.n: Adding ٱلّ at the beginning turns this word into a connector that refers back to something mentioned before. In this ayah, it refers back to هٰذَا (this) from the previous phrase.

steps.m: he (referring to the one being discussed in this ayah)
steps.n: This is a pronoun that refers back to someone or something mentioned before. In this ayah, it's referring to Prophet Musa (Moses), who is being spoken about disrespectfully by Pharaoh.
Added vowels and ي to show this describes someone who is affected by weakness or being despised
Added double damma to show this word is describing someone in the sentence

steps.m: and not (negates يَكَادُ يُبِينُ - 'he can hardly express')
steps.n: Added وَ at the beginning to connect this negation with the previous statement. Here it connects to negate يَكَادُ يُبِينُ (he can hardly express himself)

steps.m: to almost do something/to be on the verge of

steps.n: Connected the letters with fatha vowels. The middle letter و changed to ا because it's a weak letter.
Added يَ at the beginning to show this is happening in the present time

steps.m: he almost does/is almost doing (something) (subject of يُبِينُ)
steps.n: Added ُ at the end to show this word is doing the action in يُبِينُ (making clear/explaining)
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the past tense verb
Changes to present tense by adding يُ at start, changing vowels, and adding ُ at end

Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb

steps.m: was thrown (something that happened to أَسْوِرَةٌ - bracelets)
steps.n: Changed to passive form to show that something was thrown/cast rather than someone doing the throwing. In this ayah, it's talking about bracelets (أَسْوِرَةٌ) that were thrown/cast.

steps.m: with him (referring to Pharaoh mentioned earlier in the ayah)
steps.n: Added the attached pronoun هُ (him) to show who is being referred to

Added هُمْ at the end to show who the action was done to (them)

Changed from a past-tense verb (he went ahead) to a noun (predecessor) by adding ًا at the end to show it's the object being acted upon

steps.m: son (subject of this phrase, referring to Jesus)

Changes from singular 'god' to plural 'gods' by adding آ at start

Added fatha on first letter and sukun on second letter to make it into a noun. This word refers back to عيسى (Jesus) who is being described as Allah's servant in this verse.
Added double damma at the end to show this is the subject (doer) in the sentence 'he is nothing but a servant'
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Added hamza at beginning to make the action causative (doing something to someone else). Changed vowels to match this form.
Added نَا at the end to show 'we' did the action. Changed final vowel to sukun before the suffix.
This is the basic preposition meaning 'on' or 'upon'
The ى changes to ي with sukun when connecting to pronouns
Added هِ at the end to show this is happening to 'him' (referring back to the servant mentioned earlier in the verse)

steps.m: to make something into something else

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to show this happened in the past
Adding the suffix نا changes the subject from 'he' to 'we'
Adding و connects this phrase to what came before it
Adding هُ shows who/what was made into something

steps.m: to compare or set an example

steps.n: Basic noun form indicating an example or similitude

steps.m: an example (object of جَعَلْنٰهُ - we made him)
steps.n: Added ًا to show this is the second object after him/it in 'we made him an example'
Adding the basic vowel pattern for past tense verbs
بَنِي means 'children' (not 'he built'). Added لِ at beginning to mean 'for/to the children'
Added emphasis (shaddah) to لِ and lengthened the final ي
The first part of the name meaning servant or chosen one
Added vowel marks and elongated the ending with alif
Added the second part 'il' meaning God, making the complete name mean 'servant of God'. This is the name given to Prophet Jacob (peace be upon him).
Added vowel marks to show this is a conditional particle that introduces hypothetical scenarios - things that could happen but haven't happened yet.

steps.m: and even if (connecting to previous statement)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this condition to what came before. The combination of و + لو makes the meaning stronger, changing 'if' to 'even if'.
Added vowels and combined letters. The weak letter ي changes to ا in this form.

steps.m: we want/wish (in لَجَعَلْنَا - we would make)
steps.n: Added نَ at beginning to show 'we' are doing the action. Changed ending vowel to ُ to show present tense. The long alif (آ) is used instead of regular ا for emphasis.

steps.m: to make or set something in a certain way

steps.n: Adding fathas creates the basic past tense verb showing someone did the action
Adding نا changes 'he' to 'we' as the doers of the action
Adding ل at start adds emphasis, like saying 'surely' or 'indeed'
Basic preposition meaning 'from'
Added كم at the end to show this applies to multiple people being addressed
Added vowel marks to form the singular word for angel. The meaning connects to the root since angels have divine power/control.
This is a special plural pattern used for angel. The ا and ئ are added in the middle, and ة at the end.
The doubled م adds emphasis/intensity to the word
Added tanween to show this is the object of جَعَلْنَا (we made/placed). The alif is written with a special mark (ٰٓ) in this word.
Basic preposition showing location or containment. Here it connects with ٱلْأَرْضِ (the earth) to show where something is happening.
Added vowel marks to connect the letters together
Added ٱلْ (al) at the beginning to make it specific
Added kasra at the end because it comes after the word فِى (in/on) in the ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha to form the past tense verb
Changes from past tense (he did) to present tense (he does) by adding يَ at start

steps.m: they come after/replace/succeed (referring to angels who would succeed humans on earth)
steps.n: Changed from 'he' to 'they' by adding ون at the end to match with angels (مَّلٰٓئِكَةً) mentioned before
This is an emphasis word that makes what comes after it stronger, like saying 'truly' or 'certainly'
Added و at the beginning to connect this with what came before it
Added هُ at the end to show who/what is being talked about. The small mark ۥ after هُ is just to show it's pronounced long.
Changed from 'to know' to 'knowledge' by adding the vowel 'i' after ع and making it a noun
Added two damma marks at the end to make it 'a knowledge' instead of 'the knowledge'

steps.m: surely a knowledge (of السَّاعَةِ - the Hour)
steps.n: Added لَ at the beginning to add emphasis. This word relates to السَّاعَةِ (the Hour) in this ayah, showing it is knowledge about the Day of Judgment.
Changed و to ا and added ة to make the feminine noun meaning 'hour' or 'time'

steps.m: the Hour (referring to the Day of Judgment)
steps.n: The definite article here makes this refer specifically to the Day of Judgment, a special meaning in Islamic context
Added لِ with emphasis (shown by ّ) to mean 'for/about', and kasra at the end because it follows لِ
This is a negative particle that stops something from happening. It's like saying 'don't' in English.

steps.m: so do not (doubt about عِلْمٌ - knowledge)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to show this is a result or consequence. It connects to the previous statement about knowledge (عِلْمٌ) of the Hour, saying 'so because of this, do not doubt.'
Added vowels to connect the root letters together
Added ا and ت to show doing the action to oneself
Changed from past tense 'he doubted' to present tense 'you doubt' by adding تَ at start and changing ending
Added نَّ at the end to add emphasis and certainty
Added kasra to show how the letter is pronounced

steps.m: about it/her (referring to السَّاعَةِ - the Hour)
steps.n: Added ها which refers back to 'the Hour' (السَّاعَةِ) mentioned earlier in the ayah. This shows what is being doubted about.
Basic command form telling someone to follow

steps.m: follow! (with emphasis on doing it yourself)
steps.n: The ت merged with the previous letter and got a shadda, making it a stronger command to follow
Changed from a command (do it!) to just saying 'I follow' by changing the ending
Added و at beginning to connect this word to what came before it
Added نِ at the end to show both that multiple people are being addressed and that 'me' is the object being followed
Basic demonstrative element meaning 'here' or 'this'
Added ذا to make it specifically mean 'this' for masculine singular objects
Added alif with overbar (ٰ) and fatha mark for proper pronunciation. This word is pointing to the 'path' (صِرٰطٌ) mentioned right after it in the ayah.
Added vowels and alif to make it a complete noun meaning 'path'
Changed alif to long alif (ٰ) - this is just a writing variation that doesn't change the meaning
Added double damma to show this is the subject being described as straight/upright (مُّسْتَقِيمٌ) in this ayah
Root letters got vowels to make an adjective meaning 'naturally straight'
Added مست at the start to show actively staying on the straight path (like someone following directions)
The م has a shadda (doubled letter) to make the word stronger, like saying 'very straight' instead of just 'straight'
Added marks (sukun and tanween) to show it's a complete noun meaning 'straight path'
This is a negative particle that negates what comes after it

steps.m: and not (negates يَصُدَّنَّكُمُ - let [him] avert you)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it. This negative phrase prevents the action in يَصُدَّنَّكُمُ (let him avert you)
Basic past tense form
Adding vowel marks to make it readable and show it's past tense
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start, doubling middle letter (د), and changing vowels
Added نَّ for emphasis and changed the last vowel to fatha

steps.m: he surely blocks/prevents you all (referring to ٱلشَّيْطٰنُ blocking people)
steps.n: Added كُمُ to show who is being prevented. This word is connected to ٱلشَّيْطٰنُ (Satan) who is doing the preventing.

steps.m: to be far away in rebellion
steps.n: Root meaning relates to distance and rebellion

steps.m: one who is rebelliously far away
steps.n: Root meaning 'far away in rebellion' becomes a noun meaning 'one who rebels' by adding ي between first two letters
Adding ا makes the word stronger, showing this is someone who is very rebellious
The ش gets doubled (shadda) because 'the' (ال) was added before it
Added the definite article ال to specify this particular devil/satan

steps.m: the satan/devil (subject of يَصُدَّنَّكُمُ - prevents you)
steps.n: Added damma to show this is the subject doing the action of preventing in يَصُدَّنَّكُمُ
This is an emphasis word that makes what comes after it stronger and more certain
Added هُۥ (meaning 'he') to show who the word is talking about. This 'he' refers back to Satan (الشَّيْطٰنُ) mentioned earlier in the ayah.
Added fatha to show the basic pronunciation of the preposition

steps.m: for you all (being addressed by الشَّيْطٰنُ)
steps.n: Added كُمْ to show this is directed at multiple people being spoken to. This word connects to الشَّيْطٰنُ (Satan) who is addressing these people.
Added vowels to make the word mean 'one who does the action'. The fatha (َ) and damma (ُ) pattern shows who does something.

Added a double mark (ٌّ) at the end to show this is a strong characteristic - Satan isn't just someone who happens to be hostile, but is a dedicated enemy. This word describes Satan (الشَّيْطٰنُ) in this ayah.

steps.m: to make clear or show plainly
steps.n: This root became a preposition that shows something is in the middle of other things
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb
Changed to active participle form showing who/what does the action
The م is doubled because it comes after a vowel at the end of the previous word - it doesn't change the meaning
Added tanween to show this is describing عَدُوٌّ (enemy) in the ayah, indicating the enemy is clearly apparent
This is a time particle that introduces an event that happened in the past. In this ayah, it introduces when Jesus came with clear proofs.

steps.m: and when (introduces Jesus coming with clear proofs)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this event with what came before it in the story.
Added vowel marks (fatha) to make it mean 'he came' in the past tense
Changed to hollow verb pattern by adding alif after first letter
This is a proper noun (name) that comes from the Hebrew/Aramaic name Yeshua (Jesus). It doesn't have an Arabic root.
Added the ى with fatha to make the proper Arabic form of the name
Added the small alif (ٰ) above the ى which helps show the correct pronunciation
Root letters with extra 'ي' to make it stronger - means VERY clear/obvious

The word changed from singular to plural by adding ات (aat) at the end. This makes it mean 'multiple clear proofs' instead of just one clear proof. The word is connected to 'came' in the sentence through the word 'with'.
The letter ب is added to show this is done 'with' something. It connects to the verb before it.
The word means 'clear proofs' and comes after the word 'with' (بِ), showing what Jesus brought with him
Added fatha marks to show this is past tense and means 'he did it'

steps.m: he said (subject of جِئْتُكُم - I have come to you)
steps.n: The middle letter و changes to ا because it has a fatha before it. This is a common change in Arabic that doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: indeed/certainly (emphasizing جِئْتُكُم - I have come to you)
steps.n: This is a particle that adds emphasis to verbs. When used before a past tense verb like جِئْتُكُم (I have come to you), it confirms that the action has definitely happened.
The root letters were given vowels to make the command 'come!' - the middle letter changed to hamza (ئ) because it has a kasra before it
Added تُ at the end to show that 'I' did this action in the past
Added كُم at the end to show this action was done to 'you all'
Connected the root letters with fatha to create the basic past tense verb
Changes from 'he judged' to 'wisdom' by adding ة and changing vowels
Added the preposition بِ to show how something is done
Added ال to make it specific wisdom
Added kasra at the end because it follows the preposition بِ

steps.m: to make something clear or evident

steps.n: Changed to command form by adding vowels - the line under ي (kasra) and line above ن (fatha) tell us this is asking someone to make something clear
Changes to 'I' form (first person) - not passive voice
Added لِ to show purpose - 'in order to' or 'so that'

steps.m: and so that I make clear
steps.n: Wāw connects this clause to previous statement
Added fatha to the letter lam to show it's a preposition meaning 'for' or 'to'
Added the pronoun كُم which means 'you all'. This word is receiving the action of أُبَيِّنَ (I make clear) in this ayah.
Added fatha and sukun to form the basic noun pattern
Added fatha at the end because this word is being acted upon by the verb أُبَيِّنَ (I make clear). It's like saying 'I will make clear some (of what you differ about)'.
This is the basic form of the relative pronoun that connects parts of sentences, like 'who' or 'which' in English
Added ٱلّ at the beginning to make it more specific. In this ayah, it connects to the word تَخْتَلِفُونَ (you differ), showing what they differ about.

steps.m: to differ or come after something

steps.n: Connected the root letters with basic past tense vowels
Added letters make it mean 'to disagree with each other' instead of just 'differ'
Changes from past tense 'he disagreed' to present tense 'you/she disagrees' by adding تَ at start and changing vowels
Changes to plural 'you all' form
This is the basic preposition في which shows location or containment

steps.m: in it (referring to what they differ about)
steps.n: Added the pronoun ه (him/it) to show what is being referred to. In this ayah, it refers back to the matters they differ about.

steps.m: to protect or guard against harm

steps.n: Connected the root letters with basic past tense vowels
Added اِتَّ at the start to show protecting oneself (like adding 'self' in English). The و disappeared when combined with ت
Added فَ to connect this with the previous statement about Jesus bringing clear proofs
Changed from 'he was mindful' to 'be mindful!' by adding وا at the end to make it a command for a group
Added ال to specify this is THE one true God, and merged the letters together with special spelling for this unique divine name

steps.m: Allah (as the object being feared in فَٱتَّقُوا۟)
steps.n: Allah is the one being feared - the fatha (small line) at the end shows this
Changed from root letters to past tense verb by adding vowels
Changed from 'he obeyed' to 'I obey' by adding أ at start and changing vowels
Changed from 'I obey' to a command for many people by adding وا at the end
Added conjunction 'and'
Added object pronoun 'me'
This is the basic form of the word that can mean 'if' or 'whether'

steps.m: indeed/truly (emphasizing that اللَّهَ is my Lord and your Lord)
steps.n: Adding the shadda changes this from a conditional word to one that strongly emphasizes what comes after it. Here it emphasizes that Allah is both the speaker's Lord and the listeners' Lord.
Added ال to specify this is THE one true God, and the hamza was dropped because this is a special divine name
The word 'Allah' gets a stronger L sound (shadda), connects smoothly to the word before it, and ends with -a because it follows 'inna' (indeed)

This is a basic pronoun that refers back to Allah mentioned in إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ (Indeed, Allah). It connects the previous statement about Allah to what comes after, showing that the same subject is being discussed.

steps.m: to care for and develop something to completion

steps.n: Root letters combined with shadda (double letter) to make 'lord' - someone who takes care of others
Added kasra at the end to show this word connects to what comes after it
Added ى at the end to show this is my Lord. This word is being described by هُوَ (He) in this ayah.

steps.m: to care for and help grow/develop

steps.n: Added وَ (meaning 'and') at the start + the root letters form رَبّ (lord/master who cares for others)
Added ُ (damma) at the end to show this is the subject/doer in the sentence
Added كُمْ at the end to show this belongs to 'you all'
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Changed vowels to make it a command form (do this!)
Added helper alif at start to make pronunciation easier
Added وا to make it plural command
Added هُ meaning 'him' to show who to worship
Added فَ at start to mean 'so/then'
This is the basic attention-getting word that forms the start of pointing words
The alif is written above the ه to show it's specifically about drawing attention to something present

steps.m: this (pointing to the straight path that will be mentioned)
steps.n: Adding ذَا makes it specifically point to something nearby. In this ayah, it's pointing forward to the 'straight path' that will be mentioned next.
Adding the basic vowels to make the word readable
The small line above the ر makes you stretch out the 'aa' sound when saying 'path'
Added tanween damma to show this is indefinite and is described by the word مُّسْتَقِيمٌ (straight) that follows it
Added vowels to connect the root letters
Added مُسْتَ at start to show something is firmly/continuously straight, like a straight path that stays straight
Added tanween (double damma) at end to show this is any straight path, not a specific one
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added اِ at start and ت after first letter to show people disagreeing with each other (mutual action)

steps.m: and then they differed (with each other)
steps.n: Added فَ at beginning to show this happened as a result of or after something else

steps.m: to gather or form a group

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the letters together and show this happened in the past
Changed the vowel pattern to turn the action into a noun meaning the result of that action
Added أ at the start and ا after ز to make it mean 'many groups' instead of just one group
Added ال at the beginning to make it specific and ُ at the end to show it's the subject
This is a preposition that shows where something starts from or connects parts of a sentence. Here it connects 'punishment' (عَذَابِ) to 'they wronged' (ظَلَمُوا).
Added a small ن (called nun) at the end to help with pronunciation when connecting to the next word
Changed from basic root letters to a common word meaning 'between' by adding the vowels a and silent y
Added kasra at the end because this word is connected to something after it
Added هِمْ at the end to show this means 'between them'
This is a word used to warn of something terrible that will happen. It's like saying 'terrible punishment' or 'destruction' in English.
The ٌ at the end shows this is the main subject of what's being said

steps.m: so woe (connected to فَٱخْتَلَفَ before it)
steps.n: The فَ at the beginning shows this is happening because of what came before - they disagreed (فَٱخْتَلَفَ), so woe to them!
This is a combination of two parts: لِ meaning 'for' and الَّذِي meaning 'the one who'. The ا from ال is dropped when combined.
Added نَ at the end to make it plural, meaning 'those' instead of 'the one'. This word connects to ظَلَمُوا۟ (they wronged) in the ayah.

steps.m: to act unfairly or harm others

steps.n: Connected the letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added وا at the end to show that a group of people did this action. This word describes who will receive وَيْلٌ (woe/destruction) in this ayah.
Basic preposition showing where something starts or comes from. In this ayah, it connects 'the groups' (ٱلْأَحْزَابُ) with 'among them' (بَيْنِهِمْ) to show where the disagreement happened.

steps.m: to punish severely or cause pain

steps.n: Changes the action into a noun meaning the result of punishment
Added kasra at the end to show this punishment belongs to or is connected to the word يَوْمٍ (a day) that comes after it
Connected the letters with fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to form the basic noun
Added tanwin kasra to show this word is indefinite (a day) and is possessed by another word (عَذَابِ - punishment)
Added vowel marks to make it into an adjective describing something that causes pain
Added ي to intensify the meaning, making it describe something extremely painful
Added tanwin kasra to show this word describes عَذَابِ (punishment) in the phrase 'punishment of a severely painful day'
This is a question word that always comes at the start of a sentence to turn it into a yes/no question

steps.m: do/does/did (asking if they are waiting for the Hour)
steps.n: Added a sukun (ْ) at the end to show where to stop when reading. In this ayah, it's asking if people are waiting for the Hour (Day of Judgment).
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the past tense verb
Changes from past to present by adding يَ at start and changing middle vowel to ُ
The word changed from 'he looks' to 'they look' by adding ون at the end - this makes it plural (more than one person looking)
This is the base form of the word that means 'except'. It's used to exclude something from what was mentioned before it.

Adding the shadda (ّ) and alif (ا) makes the exclusion stronger and clearer. In this ayah, it's excluding 'the Hour' from what people are waiting for, connecting to السَّاعَةَ (the Hour) that comes after it.

steps.m: to measure time or move through space

steps.n: The middle letter و changes to ا to make a word meaning 'time period' from the root meaning 'to measure time'
Adding ة makes it into a specific unit of time

steps.m: the Hour (referring to the Day of Judgment)
steps.n: Adding ٱل makes it specific and doubles the س because it's a sun letter. The َ at the end shows it's the object of يَنظُرُونَ (they await)
This is a connecting word that links two ideas together. Here it connects 'they are waiting for' (يَنظُرُونَ) with 'it comes to them' (تَأْتِيَهُم), showing what they are waiting for.
Connected the root letters together to form the base word
Added present tense prefix and vowels. This refers to السَّاعَةَ (the Hour) coming in this ayah.

steps.m: it comes to them (السَّاعَةَ comes to them)
steps.n: Added pronoun suffix هُم meaning 'them' and connecting vowel َ
Added fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to make it into a noun meaning 'sudden'
Adds ة at the end to mean 'one sudden event' instead of just 'sudden'

steps.m: suddenly (describing how تَأْتِيَهُم/it comes to them)
steps.n: Added tanween (ً) to show this describes how something happens. This word describes how the Hour (ٱلسَّاعَةَ) comes to them in the phrase تَأْتِيَهُم (it comes to them).
Added diacritics to show the word's pronunciation and grammatical role as a subject pronoun
Added و at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it. This pronoun 'they' is the subject of the verb لَا يَشْعُرُونَ (they do not perceive)
Basic negative particle that makes the verb after it negative. In this ayah, it negates يَشْعُرُونَ (they perceive), making it mean 'they do not perceive'
Added fatha mark on ل to show correct pronunciation

steps.m: to perceive or be aware of something

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the past tense form
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing middle vowel to damma

steps.m: they perceive (referring to those who deny the Hour)
steps.n: Added ون to show multiple people doing the action. This refers to the disbelievers mentioned earlier in the ayah who don't expect the Hour.

steps.m: to be close friends with someone

steps.n: Adds ي between second and third root letters to show someone who has the quality of being a close friend
Changes the word from singular (one close friend) to plural (many close friends) by adding أ at the start and آء at the end

steps.m: the close friends (subject of يَوْمَئِذٍۭ بَعْضُهُمْ لِبَعْضٍ عَدُوٌّ)
steps.n: Adds ال at the beginning to show we're talking about specific close friends. This word is the subject of the phrase 'on that Day some of them will be enemies to others'
Adds a u-sound (ُ) at the end to show this word is doing the action
Connected the letters together and added vowel marks to make the basic noun 'day'

steps.m: on that day (when friends become enemies, as mentioned in the surrounding ayah)
steps.n: Added ئِذٍ which means 'that' or 'then' to specify which day we're talking about. The ٍ at the end shows this word is connected to what comes after it in the sentence.

steps.m: to separate or divide something into parts

steps.n: This turns the concept of dividing into a noun meaning 'some' or 'a part'. The ٌ shows it's a subject.
The word connects to هُمْ (them) so we remove the tanween (double damma) and keep a single damma
Adding هُمْ (them) to show who 'some' refers to

steps.m: to be part of something or separate into parts

steps.n: Added vowel marks to make the root letters into a noun meaning 'some' or 'part'. This word is connected to بَعْضُهُمْ (some of them) in the ayah.
Added لِ at the beginning to show direction or purpose - this shows who the action is directed towards
Added tanween kasra (ٍ) at the end to show this is indefinite (not talking about a specific part) and is affected by the word before it
Added vowels to make it into a noun meaning someone who does the action

steps.m: enemy (subject of يَوْمَئِذٍۭ - on that day)
steps.n: Added double damma and shadda to show this is someone who is intensely or professionally hostile - an enemy. This word describes how friends will become enemies to each other on that day, except for the righteous.
This is a basic particle that means 'except'. It doesn't come from a root.
Added shadda and alif to complete the word. In this ayah, it shows an exception to the previous statement about friends becoming enemies - the God-fearing will remain friends.

steps.m: one who guards against/is mindful (active participle)
steps.n: Changes from verb (to guard) to noun meaning 'one who guards'
Added ين to show this refers to multiple people
Added ٱلْ to specify these particular people, and َ to show this word is being acted upon in the sentence
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Changed from a verb (he worshipped) to a plural noun (worshippers) by changing the vowels and pattern
Added يٰ at the beginning to show Allah is calling out to His servants, and ِ at the end to show possession
Basic negative particle that negates the fear (خَوْفٌ) being upon them (عَلَيْكُمُ) in this ayah
Added fatha mark on ل to show correct pronunciation
Changed the verb into a noun by adding fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter. This creates the basic noun meaning 'fear'.
Added tanween damma to show this is the subject after لَا (no). The word means 'any fear' because it's indefinite.
The ى changes to يْ when we add something to the end of the word. This is just a spelling change and doesn't change the meaning.
كُمُ shows this applies to multiple people being spoken to directly. In this ayah, Allah is addressing His servants (يٰعِبَادِ) directly.
Added fatha and sukun to make the basic noun 'day'

steps.m: today (when there will be no fear)
steps.n: Added ال to make it specific and fatha at the end to show it's describing when something happens. In this ayah, it's telling us when there will be no fear.
The alif is written with a special mark (آ) to show it's pronounced longer, but the meaning stays the same

steps.m: and not (connected to لَا خَوْفٌ - no fear)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it. This connects 'no fear' with 'and not you will grieve'
This is the basic form of the pronoun meaning 'you'
Added ت to make the pronoun stronger and clearer
Added م with sukun to show this refers to multiple people. This pronoun is the subject of the verb تَحْزَنُونَ (you will grieve) in this ayah.

steps.m: to raise, elevate, or build something high
